title: hydrangea maritima
intro: Hydrangea maritima, also known as the sea lavender or coast hydrangea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Hydrangeaceae family. It is a low-growing, shrubby perennial that is native to coastal areas in Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Hydrangea maritima can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive problems.
